description: Seed patterns for the audit/supply-chain family. Hits target install / post-install hooks, release workflows, publish credentials, and unpinned dependency surfaces. Most of the work happens in manifest, CI, and release-config files rather than source code, so the lang_groups here map to ecosystem-specific artefact globs (manifest_node, ci_workflow, etc.), not to source-language extensions.
notes: Most hits will point at JSON / YAML / TOML files; the downstream skill should read the entire file, not just the matched line.
Absence of a lockfile is itself a finding for this family — but ripgrep cannot detect absence. The skill needs to confirm presence/absence via Glob.
Release workflows that reference `secrets.X_TOKEN` are not vulnerabilities on their own; the skill must check scoping (per-branch / per-environment / per-workflow).

manifest_node.description: npm install / lifecycle script declared in package.json — runs on every install of this package
manifest_node.false_positive_notes: Hook scripts can be legitimate (build steps); the skill must read the command they run.

manifest_node.description: package.json marked private — NOT published to npm (negative signal for publish-side risk)
manifest_node.false_positive_notes: When present, the package never ships to a registry; publish-workflow concerns deprioritised.

manifest_node.description: package.json declares an executable shipped to user PATH — install-time exposure
manifest_node.false_positive_notes: Common in CLIs; not a defect, but worth knowing what the binary actually does.

manifest_python.description: setup.py customising commands — runs Python during pip install
manifest_python.false_positive_notes: Legitimate for build-time generation; verify the command does not exfiltrate.

manifest_python.description: Custom Install / PostInstall / Develop command class in setup.py
manifest_python.false_positive_notes: Rare; usually means the package does something at install time.

manifest_python.description: pyproject.toml build-system.build-backend — which build engine executes
manifest_python.false_positive_notes: Standard for PEP 517 builds; investigate non-stock backends.

manifest_python.description: Python entry-points exposed by the package
manifest_python.false_positive_notes: Common; worth knowing what the entry-points do.

manifest_php.description: Composer lifecycle scripts — run on composer install / update
manifest_php.false_positive_notes: Often used for clearing caches; read the actual command.

manifest_rust.description: Cargo build.rs script declaration or build-dependencies — runs during `cargo build`
manifest_rust.false_positive_notes: Build scripts are common in low-level crates; verify the script does not fetch / execute network resources.

manifest_rust.description: Cargo.toml `publish = false` — crate not published to crates.io (negative signal)

manifest_swift.description: Package.swift dependency tracking a BRANCH (vs version) — non-reproducible
Podfile source over HTTP — MITM dependency confusion

manifest_csharp.description: NuGet.config HTTP feed — MITM dependency confusion
manifest_csharp.false_positive_notes: Critical: attacker MITM can serve malicious packages.

manifest_csharp.description: .csproj PackageReference without explicit Version attribute — non-reproducible
.NET User Secrets reference — local-only; ensure not committed
manifest_csharp.false_positive_notes: User secrets ID itself is OK; the secrets file should NOT be committed.

manifest_java.false_positive_notes: Investigate the repository URL and credential source.
Standard for Java libs. Investigate the destination repo.

manifest_java.description: SNAPSHOT dependency — non-reproducible build (any version can ship)
manifest_java.false_positive_notes: Common in dev parent POMs; concern when in release branches.

manifest_java.description: Gradle dynamic version selector (+, latest.release) — non-reproducible
manifest_java.false_positive_notes: Stable; almost always wrong outside test deps.

manifest_java.description: Maven repo over HTTP (not HTTPS) — MITM dependency confusion
manifest_java.false_positive_notes: Critical: attacker MITM can serve malicious jars.

manifest_java.description: Gradle allowInsecureProtocol — explicitly accepts HTTP repos
manifest_java.false_positive_notes: Should always be false in prod builds.

manifest_java.description: Maven plugin without <version> pin — non-reproducible build

ci_workflow.pattern: \b(?:npm publish|pnpm publish|yarn publish|cargo publish|twine upload|pypi-publish|gh release create|goreleaser)\b
ci_workflow.false_positive_notes: Investigate the trigger condition (push to tag, manual, push to main).

ci_workflow.description: Reference to a GitHub Actions secret
ci_workflow.false_positive_notes: Not a defect; the skill must check that the secret is scoped (per-environment / per-branch).
Used to anchor where each workflow's triggers live.

ci_workflow.description: GitHub Actions `pull_request_target` — runs in the target repo's context with secrets; risky on PRs from forks
ci_workflow.false_positive_notes: Use with caution is the entire point; flag for review.

ci_workflow.description: Workflow grants write permissions — broader blast radius if compromised
ci_workflow.false_positive_notes: Legitimate for release workflows; verify least-privilege at job level.

release_config.description: GoReleaser output targets — what artefacts ship
release_config.false_positive_notes: Lists artefact pipelines; investigate signing / checksum config.
